claim_bullets.bullet_text: Recursion is the root of computation, trading description for time—may be illustrated by Alan Perlis’s epigram (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.109-125).
A list can be defined as an element concatenated with a list—might be demonstrated by the array literal and spread syntax (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.109-125).
The spread operator can express list construction, allowing recursive definitions of length, map, and flatten functions—might be seen in the examples (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.109-125).

source_record_text: This chunk introduces recursion as the root of computation and explores self‑similar data structures, linear recursion, tail‑call optimization, and default arguments in JavaScript.
